introSection: Little Dipper was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Amusement Park Drive In in Laurel, Montana, USA. It was manufactured by the Allan Herschell Company in 1958, however its location prior to 2001 wasn't known. In 2001, it opened at Family Funland, but later closed in 2005. It resurfaced in 2009 at its current location. The ride only operated prior to film showings.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride circles the large projection screen used for showing films.

fullText:
Little Dipper was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Amusement Park Drive In in Laurel, Montana, USA. It was manufactured by the Allan Herschell Company in 1958, however its location prior to 2001 wasn't known. In 2001, it opened at Family Funland, but later closed in 2005. It resurfaced in 2009 at its current location. The ride only operated prior to film showings.

The ride circles the large projection screen used for showing films.
